_meta.produced_by: Claude (Anthropic) — MK Series DRS revision build

paper_title: On the Decision to Act: Strategic Convergence and the Mathematics of Intervention Timing at System Tipping Points

phase_ready.note: PH-MK5.1: AMOC empirical test (Grok Work Order 001) pending. PH-MK5.2: Theorem 4 sigma_tau scaling derivation sketch present but full non-asymptotic bound not yet independently verified. PH-MK5.3: Seven-tradition convergence is motivational evidence, not part of mathematical foundation — empirical structural analysis pending.

falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-9 — Fracttalix P2 Build Plan S48

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: kappa = 0.50 as symmetric special case of Theorem 2
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: kappa*(sym) = C_late / (C_late + C_fp) with C_late = C_fp → kappa* = 0.50. Extension of MK-P1 C-MK1.3 derivation, not a correction.
Derived from order statistics: E[min(T_1,...,T_k)] = (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k) for exponential(lambda) detection times. Theorem 3 — standard result.
Kramers-type escape rate theory near fold bifurcation — Gardiner (2004 §5.2), Berglund & Gentz (2006 Ch.5).
Derived from sigma_tau / mu_tau → ∞ as mu(t) → mu_c. t_trap is defined as the point at which E[remaining window] < agent's decision horizon T_decision.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:A]
claim_registry.name: Sequential decision theory foundation
claim_registry.statement: Sequential probability ratio test (Wald 1947) and Bayesian sequential analysis (Arrow et al. 1949; DeGroot 1970) provide the mathematical infrastructure for optimal stopping under competing hypotheses. Optimal thresholds minimize expected loss.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:A]
claim_registry.name: Critical slowing down near fold bifurcation
claim_registry.statement: Near a fold bifurcation, the dominant eigenvalue approaches zero, causing critical slowing down: rising variance and increasing autocorrelation in observable time series. DFA scaling exponent alpha increases from ~0.5 toward 1.0 as the tipping point approaches (Scheffer et al. 2009; Lenton et al. 2012).

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:A]
claim_registry.name: EWS decision-theoretic gap
claim_registry.statement: The EWS literature has not provided a principled decision theory for when to act on tipping point detections. Detection and decision are distinct problems. Lade & Niiranen (2025, Proc. Royal Society B) confirm that EWS systems can paradoxically induce riskier behavior absent decision theory.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Fortuna Process
claim_registry.statement: The Fortuna Process is the stochastic process governing a system approaching a fold bifurcation under slow parameter forcing. The tipping time tau has mean mu_tau(t) and standard deviation sigma_tau(t) that evolve as the system parameter approaches the critical value mu_c.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Virtù Window
claim_registry.statement: The Virtù Window W_v(t) is the expected remaining time during which intervention is rational: W_v(t) = E[tau | data_t] - t_current. The window is actionable when W_v(t) > T_decision, where T_decision is the agent's minimum implementation lead time.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Theorem 1 Window Rationality
claim_registry.statement: Intervention is rational if and only if E[W_v(t)] > T_decision × (1 + C_fp/C_late), where C_fp is the cost of false alarm and C_late is the cost of missed transition. The symmetric case C_fp = C_late reduces to E[W_v(t)] > T_decision.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Bayesian decision theory: act when expected benefit > expected cost. Benefit = C_late × P(transition within W_v). Cost = C_fp × P(false alarm).
inference_rule_steps.output: Rationality condition: C_late × P(transition) > C_fp × P(false alarm).

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — P(transition within T_decision) ≈ T_decision / E[W_v(t)] for small T_decision. Rationality condition becomes E[W_v] > T_decision × (1 + C_fp/C_late).

WHERE.rationality_condition_incorrect: boolean — true if there exists a cost structure (C_fp, C_late) and window distribution for which Theorem 1 condition gives a different intervention recommendation than direct expected utility calculation from Bayesian decision theory

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Specify cost structure (C_fp, C_late) and T_decision; (2) specify distribution of W_v; (3) compute direct EU(act) and EU(wait); (4) compute Theorem 1 condition; (5) check whether they agree on act/wait. Test for 10 representative cost structures. Terminates in finite steps.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-MK5.1 — approximation P(transition within T_decision) ≈ T_decision/E[W_v] is valid for T_decision << E[W_v]. For T_decision comparable to E[W_v], exact calculation required.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: Set C_fp = 0 (costless false alarms): condition becomes E[W_v] > T_decision, trivially satisfied. But C_fp = 0 is degenerate. Non-vacuous cases have C_fp > 0 where the threshold is genuinely restrictive.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Theorem 2 Asymmetric Loss Threshold
claim_registry.statement: The optimal detection threshold under asymmetric loss is delta_c* = C_late/(C_late + C_fp). The symmetric case r = 1 gives delta_c*(sym) = 0.50, recovering MK-P1's kappa = 0.50. For r > 1 (C_late > C_fp), the optimal threshold is strictly below 0.50.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — expected loss: E[Loss(delta_c)] = C_fp × P(FP | delta_c) + C_late × P(FN | delta_c). Minimize over delta_c.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — differentiating and setting to zero: delta_c* = C_late/(C_late + C_fp).
IR-3: Logical deduction — with C_fp = C_late: delta_c* = 0.50. Recovers MK-P1 kappa = 0.50 as symmetric special case.

WHERE.optimal_threshold: scalar in [0,1] — the delta_c that minimizes E[Loss(delta_c)] = C_fp × FP_rate(delta_c) + C_late × FN_rate(delta_c), computed numerically for test case (C_fp=1.0, C_late=3.0)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Specify C_fp=1.0, C_late=3.0; (2) define FP_rate and FN_rate as functions of delta_c; (3) compute E[Loss] over grid 0 to 1; (4) find minimizer; (5) compare to formula: 3.0/4.0 = 0.75; (6) check abs difference < 0.001. Terminates in finite steps.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-MK5.2 — intended extension of MK-P1 C-MK1.3, not a correction. The symmetric case is preserved. Organizations should assess actual C_fp/C_late ratio before using kappa = 0.50.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: Set C_late = 0: delta_c* = 0 (always act). Degenerate. Non-trivial regime is C_late > 0, C_fp > 0 where threshold formula gives value in (0,1). Non-vacuous.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Theorem 3 Distributed Detection Advantage
claim_registry.statement: With k independent monitoring nodes each with exponential(lambda) detection time distribution, E[min(T_1,...,T_k)] = (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k). The detection window extension relative to a single node is (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k), saturating at 1/lambda as k → ∞.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — order statistics: for k i.i.d. Exp(lambda) variables, E[min(T_1,...,T_k)] = 1/(k × lambda). Single node: E[T_1] = 1/lambda.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — window extension = E[T_1] - E[min] = (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k).
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — as k → ∞: (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k) → 1/lambda. Saturation confirmed.
inference_rule_steps.output: Saturation property: benefit concentrated in first several nodes.

WHERE.simulated_mean_min: scalar — empirical mean of min(T_1,...,T_k) from 100,000 Monte Carlo trials with T_i ~ Exp(lambda=1.0) for k in {2, 5, 10}
WHERE.formula_value: scalar — (1/lambda)(1 - 1/k) with lambda=1.0 for each k

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) For each k in {2, 5, 10}: simulate 100,000 draws of min(T_1,...,T_k) with T_i ~ Exp(1); (2) compute empirical mean; (3) compute formula (1 - 1/k); (4) check relative error < 5%. 300,000 total iterations.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: abs(simulated_mean_min - formula_value) <= 0.05 × formula_value → NOT FALSIFIED for all tested k.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-MK5.3 — independence assumption (k nodes detect independently) is critical. If nodes share a detection channel or observe correlated signals, E[min] may differ.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: For k=1: extension = 0. No extension from a single node — trivially correct. For k=2: extension = 0.5/lambda — a substantive non-zero prediction that can be tested. The k=1 degenerate case confirms non-vacuity of the k > 1 cases.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Theorem 4 Self-Generated Friction — t_trap existence
claim_registry.statement: As a system approaches its tipping point mu_c under slow parameter forcing, sigma_tau(t) ~ (mu_c - mu(t))^(-1/2) (Kramers scaling), causing CV_tau = sigma_tau/mu_tau → ∞. There exists a point t_trap before the tipping time beyond which E[remaining Virtù Window] < T_decision, making intervention irrational regardless of cost structure.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-1: Cited theorem application — Gardiner (2004 §5.2): near fold bifurcation, sigma_tau ~ (mu_c - mu)^(-1/2) (Kramers escape rate). As mu(t) → mu_c: sigma_tau → ∞.
inference_rule_steps.output: sigma_tau diverges as tipping point approaches.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — mu_tau decreases as mu(t) → mu_c, but sigma_tau diverges faster. Therefore CV_tau = sigma_tau/mu_tau → ∞.

IR-3: Logical deduction — as CV_tau → ∞, E[W_v(t)] → 0 relative to T_decision. There exists t_trap defined by E[W_v(t_trap)] = T_decision × (1 + C_fp/C_late). After t_trap, Theorem 1 rationality condition fails regardless of cost structure.
inference_rule_steps.output: t_trap existence proved conditional on Kramers scaling.

WHERE.t_trap_nonexistent: boolean — true if, for a simulated system with analytically known fold bifurcation at mu_c, the quantity E[W_v(t)] / T_decision remains > (1 + C_fp/C_late) for all t up to the empirical tipping time, meaning the rationality condition is never violated before transition

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Simulate fold bifurcation normal form: dx = (mu(t) - x^2)dt + sigma dW, with mu(t) increasing from -1 to 0 linearly; (2) at each t, estimate E[W_v(t)] from 10,000 Monte Carlo forward trajectories; (3) compute rationality ratio E[W_v(t)] / (T_decision × (1 + C_fp/C_late)); (4) check whether ratio drops below 1.0 before tipping time. If never drops: t_trap_nonexistent = true → FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-MK5.4 — PLACEHOLDER PH-MK5.2: full non-asymptotic bound on sigma_tau scaling from Berglund & Gentz (2006) not yet independently verified for this parameter regime. PH-MK5.1: AMOC empirical test registered (Grok Work Order 001) — pending bioRxiv DOI confirmation.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A system with fold bifurcation at mu_c = ∞ (no tipping point) would have E[W_v(t)] → ∞ and t_trap would not exist — t_trap_nonexistent = true → FALSIFIED. The t_trap claim is specifically about systems WITH finite tipping points. Non-trivial: requires sigma_tau to diverge faster than mu_tau decreases.

Decision trigger for adaptive action when signal emerges
